why do hunters pattern their shotguns? Patterning a shotgun is an essential step for hunters to understand how their firearm performs. It involves firing the shotgun at a target to observe how the shot spreads and where the pellets hit relative to the point of aim. This process helps hunters determine the optimal load, choke, and firing distance to ensure accurate and humane shots in the field. By patterning their shotgun, hunters can make informed choices about shell selection and shooting techniques, ultimately improving their success rate and reducing the likelihood of wounded game. why do hunters pattern their shotguns?
